projects.title: Skate Missions screen
projects.desc: Started by auditing the existing screen layout and identifying where things were breaking across different resolutions and states. Rebuilt the panel structure to be more flexible, then worked through the reskin making sure the visual updates played nicely with all the different mission states (locked, active, selected, etc.). Localisation pass involved stress testing text containers with longer strings and adjusting anchoring and truncation so nothing clipped or overlapped. Tested across stretched and non-standard aspect ratios to make sure everything stayed clean.

projects.title: Golf Clash
projects.desc: Worked through the main menu reskin keeping everything consistent with Golf Clash's existing style.Worked through the main menu reskin keeping everything consistent with Golf Clash's existing style. The chest slots sidebar was a brand new addition so laid that out from scratch, getting all the slot states in and making sure the whole thing sat correctly across different mobile resolutions.

projects.title: Point-Click Navigation System
projects.desc: Personal project. Took the UE5 top-down template and built a custom navigation layer on top. Click anywhere and a spline path appears with animated arrow decals showing the direction of travel.
projects.workflow: The template handled basic click-to-move so I focused on the path visualiser. Built BP_Navigation to grab the cursor world position and construct a spline to the target. Instead of scrolling UVs, I wrote a function that calculates the spline length at runtime, figures out how many decals are needed, and places them evenly along it. The decals physically move along the spline each tick to create the animation, and I reused the same pool of meshes rather than spawning and destroying every frame, basically object pooling to keep it performant.

media.caption: Gameplay demo — point-click navigation with animated path

projects.title: SDF Item Slot Material
projects.desc: A master UI material for item slots and selection states in UE5. One material handles multiple slot shapes, border styles and selection highlights, all swappable via parameters. Built entirely in the material graph using SDF math with zero texture samples.
projects.workflow: The goal was to avoid a separate material per item slot style. Used SDF shapes in the material graph to define the slot boundary, border and corner decorations procedurally. Parameters control the shape type, border width, corner radius, colour and selection state so designers can create any slot variant as a material instance without touching the graph. Ended up at 141 pixel shader instructions covering all variants from one master.

media.caption: SDF Item Slot System, one master material
Slot variants, rounded rect, circle, corner brackets and selection borders
Material graph, 141 pixel shader instructions, zero texture samples

projects.title: Procedural Sci-Fi Tank
projects.desc: A procedural Houdini Digital Asset built in 2022 for a sci-fi tank terminal. Fully configurable inside UE4 via Houdini Engine with exposed parameters for artists to iterate without going back to Houdini.
projects.workflow: Built the asset in Houdini and exposed key parameters directly to UE4 through Houdini Engine. Artists can adjust overall height and width, control bevel amount, set cage cut depth, toggle cutting the cage in half and add multiple screen variants, all from the details panel without touching the Houdini graph. The goal was to give the art team a flexible modular piece they could dress differently across the level without needing multiple static meshes.

media.caption: Procedural Sci-Fi Tank, live parameter editing in UE4

projects.title: Auto Landscape Material
projects.desc: A system of dynamic materials built using Material Parameter Collections and Blueprints to animate and control UI visuals in real time.
projects.workflow: Started by identifying common UI material patterns. Built a master parameter collection to drive colour, opacity, and animation globally. Created individual material functions for each effect type, then wired them into a single master material with switch parameters for artists to toggle without touching nodes.
